title: Essentialism: The Disciplined Pursuit of Less
subtitle: Do less, but better — the systematic discipline for discerning what is absolutely essential, then eliminating everything that is not.
author: Greg McKeown
excerpt: Have you ever found yourself stretched too thin? Do you feel busy but not productive? Essentialism is not about how to get more things done faster. It is a disciplined, systematic approach for determining where our highest point of contribution lies, then making execution of those things almost effortless. The way of the Essentialist is the relentless pursuit of less but better: we can make the highest possible contribution by deliberately distinguishing the vital few from the trivial many. By applying more selective criteria for where to spend our time and energy, we reclaim control of our own choices — instead of giving others the implicit permission to choose for us.
